title: Linked List — ROI & Pattern
oneSentence: Manipulate node pointers directly to reverse or merge structures.
description: Master pointer manipulation. Tracing the Reverse Linked List pattern is essential.

roi.signals: Node connections, reversing flow, merging lists, cycle detection.

codeLines.text: ListNode nextTemp = curr.next; // 1. SAVE the rest of the list

traceSteps.title: Init Pointers
traceSteps.text: Create 'prev' initialized to null, and 'curr' initialized to head.

traceSteps.title: Save Next
traceSteps.text: Temporarily save curr.next so we don't lose the rest of the list when we rewrite the pointer.

traceSteps.title: Reverse Arrow
traceSteps.text: Point curr's next arrow backwards towards prev.

traceSteps.title: Advance Prev
traceSteps.text: Slide the prev pointer forward to where curr is currently sitting.

traceSteps.title: Advance Curr
traceSteps.text: Slide the curr pointer forward to the saved nextTemp node.

traceSteps.title: Return
traceSteps.text: When curr reaches null, prev will be sitting on the new head of the reversed list.

sandbox.question: Why do we need to save curr.next in a temporary variable?
options: Because in the very next step, we overwrite curr.next to point backwards. If we didn't save it, we would lose access to the rest of the list.
To make the algorithm run in O(1) time.
It is required by Java garbage collection.
